title: Force comments automation
excerpt: When we mark a status as a certain thing or ticking a checkbox, we need to be able to ‘force’ adding comments or notes - preventing lists just becoming a ‘tick box’ exercise

posts.author: jade.woodward (Jade)
posts.cookedHtml: When we mark a status as a certain thing or ticking a checkbox, we need to be able to ‘force’ adding comments or notes - preventing lists just becoming a ‘tick box’ exercise

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: That’s a great suggestion! Have you put it on the feature suggestion board? Feature requests - monday Community Forum

posts.author: SirEric (Eric Maranga)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jade.woodward ,

Yes, you can enforce comments in Monday.com when changing statuses or checking boxes using automations. Here’s how:

  1. For Status Changes:

    • Trigger: Status changes to a specific value.
    • Condition: Check if the “Comments” column is empty.
    • Action: Reset the status and notify the user to add a comment.

  2. For Checkboxes:

    • Trigger: Checkbox is checked.
    • Condition: Check if the “Comments” column is empty.
    • Action: Uncheck the box and notify the user to add a comment.

This ensures no one can mark tasks without providing context. If you still need help setting this up, contact me at Creative Bits.

Cheers!

posts.author: jade.woodward (Jade)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for this, however, I would like to keep an audit trail of the comments from previous ‘status’ changes. The scenario is:
Item marked as a reoccuring action
Action marked as complete, where our boards then push a du date back by X months.
Next time that due date comes around, I want to force a comment/update that will not allow them to mark as complete without adding some commentary.

I believe your suggestion would just send them a polite reminder to add something, which they may or may not choose to ignore
